candidates.reasoning: The reddish-brown sandy soil, arid scrubland vegetation, and specific style of concrete block construction are highly characteristic of the Sahelian region, particularly Senegal. The flat terrain and sparse, thorny acacia-like trees are common across this coastal West African landscape.

The landscape is nearly identical to southern Mauritania, featuring the same red sandy soil and semi-arid vegetation. It is geographically indistinguishable from northern Senegal in this specific environment.

The soil color and vegetation patterns are consistent with the southern regions of Mali. However, the settlement density and building materials seen here are slightly more typical of the coastal Sahelian countries.
The environment matches the dry season landscape of The Gambia, which shares the same soil and flora as its surrounding neighbor, Senegal.

evidence: High sun angle suggesting tropical/subtropical latitude

candidates.reasoning: The presence of Prosopis juliflora (mesquite) and Acacia species in a semi-arid, sandy soil environment is highly characteristic of the Sahelian region. Senegal frequently exhibits this specific mix of invasive Prosopis and native scrubland near rural settlements.

The vegetation is consistent with the Saharan-Sahelian transition zone. The sparse, thorny scrub and drought-resistant shrubs are identical to those found in southern Mauritania, making it botanically indistinguishable from northern Senegal in this view.

The arid scrubland and presence of Acacia-type trees are common across the Sahelian belt into Mali, though the soil color and density of the scrub are slightly more typical of the coastal-adjacent Sahel.

The combination of the specific Google camera generation (Gen 3/4 quality with slight blur) and the style of the concrete utility poles with simple cross-arms is common in Senegal. The use of corrugated metal fencing and specific concrete block construction is highly characteristic of rural Senegalese settlements.

The utility pole design and the prevalence of corrugated metal structures are very similar to those found in Mali. The image quality and camera rig artifacts are consistent with coverage in this region of West Africa.
The construction materials (concrete blocks and corrugated iron) and the simple utility pole infrastructure are consistent with Burkina Faso, though less distinct than the Senegalese meta.

evidence: Google Street View camera generation/image quality

country_result:
Country: Senegal
Coordinates: 14.49, -14.45
Reasoning: The combination of reddish-brown sandy soil, semi-arid scrubland with Prosopis juliflora and Acacia, and specific concrete block construction with flat roofs is highly characteristic of the Sahelian region of Senegal. Meta-evidence, including the Google Street View Gen 3 camera quality and the specific design of the concrete utility poles with single metal cross-arms, further supports Senegal over Mali or Mauritania. While the landscape is similar to southern Mauritania, the settlement density and building materials lean more towards rural Senegal.
